highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.207 Released
highlights.summary: Auto mode is now enabled by default on Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Foundry — no more manual opt-in via environment variable. This release also fixes a frustrating terminal freeze and keystroke lag that occurred when Claude streamed long lists, tables, or paragraphs.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.206 Released
highlights.summary: The new `/doctor` command can now analyze your CLAUDE.md files and suggest trimming content that Claude could infer directly from your codebase, keeping instructions lean and effective. The `/commit-push-pr` command also got smarter — it now auto-allows `git push` to your repo's configured remote without extra permission prompts.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.203 Released
highlights.summary: Claude Code now warns you before your login session expires, so background or long-running sessions won't be silently interrupted mid-task. A new grey pause badge in the footer makes it easy to see at a glance when you're in manual permission mode.

highlights.title: GhostApproval: A Trust-Boundary Gap in AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Wiz researchers identified a vulnerability class called GhostApproval, where AI coding assistants can be manipulated into approving or executing actions that appear to come from a trusted source but don't. Developers using Claude Code should review how they configure tool permissions and avoid blindly auto-approving agent actions in sensitive codebases.

highlights.title: AI Coding Assistant Is Quietly Shipping Your Secrets
highlights.summary: This article highlights how AI coding tools can inadvertently expose secrets like API keys and tokens through context windows, logs, or generated code. Beginners should make a habit of using `.env` files, adding them to `.gitignore`, and never pasting real credentials into Claude Code prompts.

highlights.title: Show HN: Abralo – Run Multiple Claude Code Agents in One Window
highlights.summary: Abralo is a free tool that lets you manage and monitor several Claude Code agents simultaneously inside a single interface, useful for parallelizing tasks like research, email drafting, and code generation. If you find yourself constantly switching between terminal tabs for different Claude sessions, this could simplify your workflow considerably.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a concise but comprehensive walkthrough of Claude Code's core features, aiming to get intermediate users productive quickly. It's a good watch if you've already done the basics and want to level up your understanding of workflows, commands, and configuration.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners: Build Website with AI (2026)
highlights.summary: A hands-on beginner tutorial showing how to use Claude Code to build a complete website from scratch using AI assistance, with no prior coding experience required. This is an ideal starting point if you're new to Claude Code and want a practical project to follow along with.

tips_of_the_day: Run `/doctor` on your CLAUDE.md file regularly — if Claude can already infer context from your codebase (folder structure, package names, framework conventions), trim those sections to keep instructions focused and token-efficient.

Never paste real API keys or credentials into a Claude Code prompt. Instead, reference environment variables by name (e.g. `$DATABASE_URL`) and let Claude generate code that reads from the environment — your secrets stay local, your code stays shareable.

If you're running long background tasks with Claude Code, keep an eye on the session expiry warning introduced in v2.1.203. Re-authenticate proactively rather than waiting for a task to fail halfway through a multi-step agent run.
